title: pelargonium × astragaloides
intro: Pelargonium × astragaloides, also known as Rose Geranium, is a hybrid plant species within the Geraniaceae family, characterized by its fragrant leaves and clusters of small, pink or white flowers. The leaves of the Rose Geranium plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, digestive issues, and menstrual problems. The health benefits of Rose Geranium include relief from anxiety, insomnia, and menopausal symptoms, as well as its ability to reduce stress and promote relaxation. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, essential oils, and tinctures, which can be taken internally or used topically to harness its medicinal properties.
